telegram.whatEchoNeeds: Upload the single file named result.json. It comes out of Telegram Desktop (Downloads → "Telegram Desktop" → DataExport_… → ChatExport_…). The one step you must not skip: in the export dialog click the format word and switch it to "Machine-readable JSON" — Echo cannot read the HTML version.

platforms.available: no — Telegram's iPhone app has no chat-export-to-file tool; export once on Telegram Desktop (a computer), then move result.json to your phone
steps: Telegram's phone app cannot export a chat to a file — there is no export button in the iOS app. Do the export once on any Windows, Mac or Linux computer (see the Desktop tab), then send result.json back to your phone.
Quickest way back: in Telegram Desktop open your own "Saved Messages" chat, drag result.json into it and send — it appears in Saved Messages on your iPhone instantly.
On your iPhone open Telegram → Saved Messages → tap result.json → tap the share icon → "Save to Files" (pick a folder you'll remember).
Alternatively AirDrop result.json from a Mac straight to your iPhone, or email it to yourself and open the attachment.
Back in Echo, tap upload and pick result.json from the Files app (or open it from Saved Messages).
platforms.fileToUpload: result.json — a single .json text file (name is exactly result.json)
platforms.getItToPhone: Easiest: drop result.json into Telegram "Saved Messages" on the computer → it appears in Saved Messages on your iPhone → tap it → share → "Save to Files". Or AirDrop from a Mac, or email it to yourself.
platforms.timeDelay: Instant to a couple of minutes for one chat; a few minutes for very large histories.
caveats: No iOS export exists — anyone telling you to tap an export button in the iPhone app is wrong.
If you only see a .html file, you forgot to switch the format to "Machine-readable JSON" — redo the export.
Telegram overwrites the export folder each run, so grab result.json before exporting another chat.

platforms.available: no — the Telegram Android app has no chat-export tool; use Telegram Desktop on a computer, then move result.json to your phone
steps: The Telegram Android app cannot export a conversation to a file. Run the export once on a computer with Telegram Desktop (see the Desktop tab), then bring result.json to your phone.
Fastest route: in Telegram Desktop open "Saved Messages", drag result.json into it and send — it appears in Saved Messages on your Android phone.
On your phone open Telegram → Saved Messages → tap result.json to download it; it lands in your Downloads/Telegram folder.
Or copy result.json onto the phone with a USB cable, Google Drive, or email-to-self.
In Echo, tap upload and choose result.json from Downloads (or open it from Saved Messages).
platforms.getItToPhone: Send result.json to yourself via Telegram "Saved Messages" (appears on the phone instantly), or use Google Drive, USB, or email-to-self.
platforms.timeDelay: Instant to a couple of minutes per chat.
caveats: The Android app's "Export" only covers whole-account data in some builds and still won't export a single chat from the phone — use Desktop.
A .html file means the format wasn't switched to JSON — redo it.
Move result.json out before exporting a second chat; Telegram reuses the same folder.

platforms.available: yes — this is the ONLY way to export. Telegram Desktop on Windows, Mac or Linux
steps: Install Telegram Desktop from telegram.org/desktop (the standalone desktop app — NOT Telegram Web in a browser, which has no export) and log in with your phone number / QR code.
In the chat list on the left, click the conversation you want to turn into a persona so it opens on the right.
Click the three-dot menu (⋮) in the top-right of that open chat, then click "Export chat history".

In the dialog, UNCHECK all media boxes (Photos, Video files, Voice messages, Video messages, Stickers, GIFs, Files) — Echo only needs the words, and unchecking them makes the file tiny and the export fast.
Find the Format line (it usually shows "HTML" by default). Click the format word and choose "Machine-readable JSON". THIS IS THE IMPORTANT STEP — Echo cannot read the HTML version.
Leave the size-limit slider alone (with media unchecked it doesn't matter). Leave the date range on the full history ("from the beginning") so Echo gets every message.
Click "Export" and wait. A progress bar runs; seconds for a normal chat, a few minutes for years of history.

When it finishes, click "Show my data" (or open the folder manually). The export lands in Downloads → "Telegram Desktop" → a folder like DataExport_17_06_2026 → ChatExport_… , and the file you need is result.json.
Move result.json somewhere safe right away (Telegram overwrites this folder on the next export).
Send result.json to your phone (Saved Messages / AirDrop / email), then upload it to Echo.
platforms.fileToUpload: result.json — the single top-level JSON file inside Downloads/Telegram Desktop/DataExport_…/ChatExport_…
platforms.getItToPhone: Drag result.json into your Telegram "Saved Messages" and send — it appears on your phone instantly. Or AirDrop (Mac→iPhone), Google Drive, USB cable, or email-to-self.
platforms.timeDelay: Instant for small chats; up to a few minutes for very large histories.
caveats: Telegram Web (web.telegram.org) and the in-browser version have NO export — you need the installed Telegram Desktop app.
Full-account export lives elsewhere (☰/Settings → Advanced → "Export Telegram data"); you don't need that — the per-chat ⋮ → "Export chat history" gives you exactly one chat.
If "Export chat history" is greyed out or missing, fully update Telegram Desktop and restart it.
If the file is result.html instead of result.json, you didn't switch the format to "Machine-readable JSON" — redo it.

platforms.available: no — Telegram Web (in a browser) has no export feature at all; install Telegram Desktop instead
steps: The browser version (web.telegram.org / Telegram Web) does not offer chat export — there is no "Export chat history" item.
Download and install the real Telegram Desktop app from telegram.org/desktop on a Windows, Mac or Linux computer.
Log in, then follow the Desktop steps to export one chat as "Machine-readable JSON".
Move result.json to your phone and upload it to Echo.
platforms.fileToUpload: result.json (produced by Telegram Desktop, not by Telegram Web)
platforms.getItToPhone: Use Telegram "Saved Messages", AirDrop, Google Drive, or email-to-self once you have result.json from the Desktop app.
platforms.timeDelay: n/a — Web can't export; the Desktop export is instant to a few minutes.
caveats: Don't confuse Telegram Web with Telegram Desktop — only the installed Desktop app can export.
Once the Desktop app is installed, everything is identical to the Desktop instructions.

generalCaveats: Telegram is the only one of Echo's sources that genuinely requires a computer once — the phone apps have no chat-export tool. You only need the computer for the export step; the upload happens on your phone.
Choose "Machine-readable JSON" in the format toggle. The default is HTML and Echo cannot read HTML — this is the single most common mistake.
You only need the text. Uncheck every media box (photos, voice, videos, stickers, GIFs, files) — it makes the file small, the export fast, and Echo learns from the words anyway.
Telegram has no message cap — it exports your entire cloud history for that chat, so leave the date range on "from the beginning".
The file you upload is always named result.json. If you see result.html, you didn't switch the format.
Telegram overwrites the same export folder on the next run, so move result.json out (or upload it) before exporting another chat.
The export contains private messages — keep result.json safe and delete it from your phone (and the computer) after Echo finishes.

troubleshooting.problem: I exported but the file is result.html, not result.json — Echo rejects it.
troubleshooting.fix: You left the format on HTML. Re-open the chat → ⋮ → "Export chat history", click the format word, pick "Machine-readable JSON", export again, and upload the new result.json.

troubleshooting.problem: There's no "Export chat history" option in the ⋮ menu.
troubleshooting.fix: You're probably on Telegram Web or an old build. Install the standalone Telegram Desktop app from telegram.org/desktop, update to the latest version, restart it, then open the chat and try the ⋮ menu again.

troubleshooting.problem: I can't find an export button anywhere in my phone's Telegram app.
troubleshooting.fix: That's expected — the iPhone and Android apps cannot export a chat to a file. Export once on a computer with Telegram Desktop, then send result.json back to your phone (Saved Messages is fastest).

troubleshooting.problem: I can't find result.json after the export.
troubleshooting.fix: It's in Downloads → "Telegram Desktop" → a dated folder like DataExport_17_06_2026 → ChatExport_… . In the app, click "Show my data" at the end of the export to jump straight there.

troubleshooting.problem: The export is huge or takes forever.
troubleshooting.fix: You left media checked. Run it again with every media box UNCHECKED — Echo only needs text, so the file becomes tiny and finishes in seconds.

troubleshooting.problem: I exported a second chat and now my first result.json is gone.
troubleshooting.fix: Telegram overwrites the same export folder each run. Always move (or upload) result.json before exporting another conversation, then re-export the chat you lost.

troubleshooting.problem: The newest messages seem missing from the export.
troubleshooting.fix: Set the date range to the full history ("from the beginning") and let the chat fully sync in Telegram Desktop before exporting. Secret Chats are device-local and are not part of the cloud export.

whatsapp.whatEchoNeeds: Upload the .zip you exported (it contains a file called _chat.txt) — or the _chat.txt by itself. Always choose "Without Media" so it stays under WhatsApp's 40,000-message cap and uploads fast.

steps: Open WhatsApp and tap into the chat with the person you want (a 1-on-1 chat works best).
Tap the person's name (or the group name) along the top to open the contact/group info page.
Scroll all the way down on that info page until you see "Export Chat".
Tap "Export Chat".
When asked, choose "Without Media". (This keeps it under the 40,000-message limit and uploads much faster. "Attach Media" caps you at the last 10,000 messages and makes a far larger file.)
Wait a few seconds while WhatsApp prepares the file — the iOS share sheet pops up.
Tap "Save to Files".
Choose a spot you'll remember, e.g. "On My iPhone" or "iCloud Drive", then tap "Save". (Tip: "Mail" lets you email it to yourself instead.)
Come back to Echo, tap Upload, and pick that .zip from the Files app. Done!
platforms.fileToUpload: A .zip named like "WhatsApp Chat - <name>.zip". Inside it is a single _chat.txt with every message. iOS may show it as a folder/zip in the Files app.
platforms.getItToPhone: Not needed — you export directly on the phone. Tap "Save to Files" during export ("On My iPhone" or "iCloud Drive"), then upload it here from the same Files app.
caveats: If "Export Chat" is missing or greyed out, that chat has "Advanced Chat Privacy" turned on (it blocks exports). Open the chat → tap the name at top → "Advanced Chat Privacy" → turn it Off, then try again.
Choose "Without Media" — "Attach Media" is capped at the last 10,000 messages.
Only messages still in the chat are exported. Cleared messages won't be in the file.
WhatsApp exports one chat at a time — there's no "export everything" option.

steps: Open WhatsApp and tap into the chat with the person you want (a 1-on-1 chat works best).
Tap the three-dot menu (⋮) in the top-right of the chat.
Tap "More".
Tap "Export chat".
When asked, choose "Without media". (This keeps it under the 40,000-message limit and uploads faster. "Include media" adds photos/voice but caps you at the last 10,000 messages.)
Wait a moment while WhatsApp prepares the file, then the share sheet appears.
Pick where to put it: "Save to Files"/"Files" (saves to Downloads), "Drive" (Google Drive), or "Gmail" to email it to yourself.
Open Echo, tap Upload, and select that file from your Downloads (or from Drive). Done!
platforms.fileToUpload: A .txt file (often shared/saved inside a .zip). The text file is the chat; inside a zip it's named _chat.txt.
platforms.getItToPhone: Not needed — you export directly on the phone. Save it to Downloads/Drive and upload here from the same phone.
caveats: If "Export chat" is missing or won't work for a chat, "Advanced Chat Privacy" is on for it. Tap ⋮ → contact/group name → "Advanced Chat Privacy" → turn it Off, then export again.
Pick "Without media" — "Include media" is capped at the last 10,000 messages and makes a large file.
Some Android share sheets label the save target differently ("Files", "My Files", "Save to device") — any is fine, just remember where it lands.
Only messages still present in the chat are exported; cleared messages won't appear.

platforms.available: no — WhatsApp Desktop and WhatsApp Web have no "Export Chat" option
steps: WhatsApp Desktop and WhatsApp Web have no Export Chat feature — this is normal, not a bug.
Pick up your phone and follow the iPhone or Android steps instead.
The export happens entirely on the phone, and since you upload to Echo from the phone too, nothing needs to go to a computer.
platforms.fileToUpload: n/a — there is no desktop export. Export from your phone instead.
platforms.getItToPhone: Nothing to move: do the export on the phone (see iPhone/Android) and upload from there. Echo is mobile, so upload straight from the phone.
caveats: Don't waste time hunting for an export button on the desktop/web app — it isn't there.
All current WhatsApp exports are phone-only.

generalCaveats: Always choose "Without Media" / "Without media". It exports up to the last 40,000 messages, makes a small file, and uploads fast. (With media, WhatsApp caps the export at the last 10,000 messages.)
Voice notes are NOT in a "Without Media" export — they only come in a with-media export (as separate .opus files). Echo reads text, so without-media is the right choice.
The result is a .zip containing one file called _chat.txt. On Android you sometimes get the .txt directly. Either works in Echo.
"Without media" replaces pictures/videos/voice with a short placeholder line (e.g. "image omitted") — that's expected and fine.
Only messages currently in the chat are exported. For a richer persona, export a long-running, active 1-on-1 chat.
Per-chat "Advanced Chat Privacy" (rolled out 2025) blocks export for that chat — if Export is greyed out, turn that setting off for the chat first.
Privacy: a chat export contains the other person's messages too. Only export and upload chats you have the right to use.

troubleshooting.problem: "Export Chat" / "Export chat" is missing or greyed out for a specific chat.
troubleshooting.fix: That chat has "Advanced Chat Privacy" enabled (it intentionally blocks exports). Open the chat → tap the contact/group name at the top → "Advanced Chat Privacy" → turn it Off, then redo the export.

troubleshooting.problem: I'm in Germany and heard export was removed.
troubleshooting.fix: WhatsApp briefly pulled export for German users back in 2020 (a patent dispute) but restored it afterward — it works again today. If a single chat still won't export, it's the per-chat "Advanced Chat Privacy" setting, not a country block: turn that off for the chat and try again.

troubleshooting.problem: The file is huge or the upload is slow / times out.
troubleshooting.fix: You almost certainly exported "With Media". Re-export and choose "Without Media" / "Without media" — it's a tiny text file and uploads in seconds.

troubleshooting.problem: I can't find an Export option in WhatsApp on my computer.
troubleshooting.fix: WhatsApp Desktop and Web don't support export at all — that's normal. Do the export on your phone (iPhone/Android steps) and upload to Echo from the phone.

troubleshooting.problem: Echo says the file isn't recognized.
troubleshooting.fix: Upload the .zip from the export, or the _chat.txt inside it — not a screenshot, PDF, or a renamed file. Don't unzip and re-zip it; upload the original .zip (or the _chat.txt) as-is.

troubleshooting.problem: Names show as phone numbers instead of names.
troubleshooting.fix: That happens when the other person isn't saved in your contacts. It still works — Echo just sees a number. Save the contact and re-export if you'd prefer the name.

troubleshooting.problem: Accented letters or emoji look garbled (mojibake) after export.
troubleshooting.fix: Upload the original .txt/_chat.txt straight from WhatsApp without opening and re-saving it in another app. WhatsApp exports proper UTF-8; re-saving in some editors can corrupt the encoding.

instagram.whatEchoNeeds: Upload the single Instagram .zip exactly as downloaded (it contains the messages JSON). Pick Format = JSON when you create it; never HTML, and never unzip it first.

steps: Open the Instagram app and tap your profile picture (bottom-right).
Tap the menu (three lines, top-right), then "Settings and privacy".
Tap "Accounts Center" near the top (the blue Meta Accounts Center panel).
Tap "Your information and permissions".
Tap "Download your information", then "Download or transfer information".
Choose your Instagram account (tap it, then "Next"). If asked, choose "Some of your information" (not "All available information").
Scroll to "Messages" and tick only its checkbox, then tap "Next".
Choose "Download to device" (fastest). If you only see "Send download link to email", that's fine too.
Set Date range to "All time".
Set Format to "JSON" (required — HTML will not work with Echo).
Set Media quality to "Low" — Echo only needs text, and Low keeps the file small and fast.
Tap "Create files" (sometimes "Start export") and confirm with your password if prompted.
Wait for the "ready to download" email/notification (a few hours, up to 48h). Open it, tap "Download", sign in if asked, and the .zip saves to your phone.
Open Echo, tap upload, and pick that .zip from Files/Downloads. Don't unzip it first.
platforms.fileToUpload: A .zip named like instagram-yourusername-2026-06-17-xxxxxxx.zip. Inside, chats live in your_instagram_activity/messages/inbox/, one folder per conversation, each with a message_1.json. Upload the whole .zip — don't unzip it.
platforms.getItToPhone: You can do the whole thing on your phone, so the file lands in Files/Downloads automatically — nothing to transfer. (If you used "Send download link to email" and opened it on a computer, save the .zip, then AirDrop it to your iPhone or email it to yourself and Save to Files, then upload that .zip.)
platforms.timeDelay: Usually a few hours, up to 48 hours. You get an email/notification when ready, then ~4 days to download before the link expires.
caveats: Format MUST be JSON — HTML exports can't be parsed.
Tick only "Messages" and set Media quality "Low" for a small, fast export.
Garbled text/emoji is normal (Meta stores these JSON files mis-encoded) — Echo fixes it automatically.

steps: Open the Instagram app and tap your profile picture (bottom-right).
Tap the menu (three lines, top-right), then "Settings and privacy".
Tap "Accounts Center" (the Meta Accounts Center panel near the top).
Tap "Your information and permissions".
Tap "Download your information", then "Download or transfer information".
Select your Instagram account and tap "Next". If prompted, choose "Some of your information".
Scroll to "Messages", tick only that box, and tap "Next".
Choose "Download to device" if offered (fastest); otherwise "Send download link to email" also works.
Set Date range to "All time".
Set Format to "JSON" (required — do not pick HTML).
Set Media quality to "Low" to keep the file small.
Tap "Create files" (or "Start export") and confirm with your password if asked.
When the "ready to download" notification/email arrives, open it, tap "Download", and the .zip saves to your Downloads folder.
Open Echo, tap upload, and choose that .zip. Leave it zipped.
platforms.fileToUpload: A .zip (e.g. instagram-yourusername-2026-06-17-xxxxxxx.zip) containing your_instagram_activity/messages/inbox/ with a message_1.json per conversation. Upload the whole .zip.
platforms.getItToPhone: Done entirely on the phone — the .zip lands in Downloads, nothing to move. (If you used the email link on a computer, download the .zip there and send it to your phone via Google Drive or email-to-self, then upload it.)
platforms.timeDelay: Usually a few hours, up to 48 hours. Email/notification arrives when ready; download link is valid ~4 days.
caveats: Format MUST be JSON — HTML can't be parsed.
Tick only "Messages" and Media quality "Low" for a small, fast export.
Garbled text/emoji is normal — Echo repairs the encoding automatically.

steps: On a computer browser, go to accountscenter.instagram.com (or instagram.com → Settings → "Accounts Center") and log in.
Click "Your information and permissions".
Click "Download your information", then "Download or transfer information".
Pick your Instagram account and click "Next". Choose "Some of your information" if asked.
Find "Messages", tick only that box, and click "Next".
Choose "Download to device", set Date range "All time", Format "JSON", Media quality "Low".
Click "Create files" and confirm your password.
When the email arrives, download the .zip on the computer, move it to your phone (AirDrop / email / Google Drive / Files), and upload that .zip to Echo.
platforms.fileToUpload: The same .zip (instagram-yourusername-…zip) with your_instagram_activity/messages/inbox/ JSON files inside. Upload the whole .zip.
platforms.getItToPhone: If you did this on a computer, save the .zip then move it to your phone: AirDrop (Mac→iPhone), email-to-self, or upload to Google Drive/iCloud and re-download in your phone's Files app. Then upload that .zip from your phone.
platforms.timeDelay: Hours up to 48h; ~4-day link expiry. A good fallback if the app's export screen misbehaves.
caveats: Web works, but the app keeps the file on your phone where Echo lives.
On a shared/work computer, sign out of Accounts Center afterward.

generalCaveats: Format MUST be JSON. HTML exports are human-readable but Echo cannot parse them.
Choose only "Messages" and Media quality "Low" — Echo needs just text. This makes the file small and the export much faster (full-media exports can take the full 48h and be huge).
The export is not instant: a few hours up to 48 hours, and the download link expires after ~4 days — grab it promptly.
Garbled text/emoji (e.g. "ð" instead of an emoji) is normal — Meta stores these JSON files mis-encoded (latin1). Echo automatically fixes the encoding on our side.

Privacy note: Instagram removed end-to-end encrypted DMs on May 8, 2026, so there is no longer a separate "secure storage"/encrypted-chat export — all your regular DMs now come through this one standard download. (If you exported encrypted chats before that date, you can upload that file too.)
Not included in any export: deleted messages, Vanish-mode messages, and unsent messages — those are gone permanently.
Only your DMs from the account you log into are included; the other person's separate copy is not.
Upload the whole .zip — Echo opens it for you. No need to unzip it or pick out files.

troubleshooting.problem: I only see "All available information", not "Some of your information".
troubleshooting.fix: Either works — but "Some of your information" lets you pick only Messages, which is far faster and smaller. If only the full option shows, continue and just make sure Format is JSON; Echo reads the messages folder and ignores the rest.

troubleshooting.problem: I picked HTML by mistake, or the file opens as web pages.
troubleshooting.fix: Echo can't read HTML. Start the export again and choose Format = JSON. The folders will then contain message_1.json files instead of .html files.

troubleshooting.problem: The .zip is huge / the export is taking the full 48 hours.
troubleshooting.fix: You probably left media in. Re-run it selecting only "Messages" and Media quality "Low" — text-only exports are small and usually finish in a few hours.

troubleshooting.problem: The download link in the email doesn't work or says expired.
troubleshooting.fix: Links expire after ~4 days. Create a new export and download it within a few days. You may need to re-enter your Instagram password to open the link.

troubleshooting.problem: Emojis and accented letters look like "Ã©" or "ð…" garbage.
troubleshooting.fix: Expected — Meta's JSON is mis-encoded (latin1). Upload it as-is; Echo automatically repairs the encoding so names, emoji and accents come out correct.

troubleshooting.problem: I can't find "Accounts Center" or "Download your information".
troubleshooting.fix: Profile → menu (three lines) → "Settings and privacy" → "Accounts Center" (top) → "Your information and permissions" → "Download your information" → "Download or transfer information". If the app hides it, do it on a computer at accountscenter.instagram.com and AirDrop/email the .zip to your phone.

facebook.whatEchoNeeds: Upload the single ZIP from Meta's "Download your information" with Messages ticked and Format = JSON (not HTML) — inside is a your_facebook_activity/messages/ folder with message_1.json files. Do NOT upload the separate "Secure storage" zip unless your chat is end-to-end encrypted.

platforms.available: yes — use the FACEBOOK app, not Messenger (Messenger's download option was removed)
steps: Open the blue Facebook app (NOT Messenger). Confirm you're on the correct account.
Tap your profile picture (top right), scroll to "Settings & privacy", then tap "Settings".
Tap "Accounts Center" near the top.
Tap "Your information and permissions".
Tap "Download your information".
Tap "Download or transfer information".
Pick your Facebook account if prompted, then tap "Next".
Choose "Some of your information".
Find and tick "Messages" only, leave everything else off, then tap "Next".
Choose "Download to device".
Set "Date range" to "All time".
Set "Format" to "JSON" — the default is HTML, so you MUST switch it to JSON.
Leave "Media quality" on "High", then tap "Create files" (or "Create export").
Wait for the notification/email that your file is ready.
Return to "Download your information", open "Available downloads", tap "Download", enter your password, and save the .zip (it goes to Files under Downloads).
Come back here and upload that .zip from Files — no need to unzip.
platforms.fileToUpload: One file named like facebook-<yourname>-<date>.zip saved to your Files app (On My iPhone / Downloads). Inside: your_facebook_activity/messages/ with message_1.json files.
platforms.getItToPhone: If you do this on the phone, the zip lands in your Files app (Downloads) — just upload it from there, no transfer needed.
platforms.timeDelay: Usually minutes; can be up to 24–72 hours for large or old accounts.
caveats: Only the Facebook app has this; the Messenger app does not.
Default format is HTML — change it to JSON or the export can't be used.
Download link expires in ~4 days.
Password re-auth is required before download.
The saved zip is in Files → Downloads (or wherever Safari/Facebook saves it); use that when uploading.

platforms.available: yes — do this in the FACEBOOK app, not the Messenger app (Messenger's own download option was removed)
steps: Open the blue Facebook app (NOT Messenger). Make sure you're logged into the right account.
Tap your profile picture (top right), scroll down and tap "Settings & privacy", then "Settings".
Tap "Accounts Center" near the top (the Meta Accounts Center).
Tap "Your information and permissions".
Tap "Download your information".
Tap "Download or transfer information".
If asked, choose your Facebook account (tap it), then tap "Next".
Choose "Some of your information".
Scroll to the messages section and tick "Messages" only. Leave everything else unticked, then tap "Next".
Choose "Download to device".
Set "Date range" to "All time".
Set "Format" to "JSON". IMPORTANT: the default is "HTML" — switch it to JSON or the file won't work.
Set "Media quality" to "High" (optional), then tap "Create files" (sometimes "Create export").
Wait for Facebook's notification/email that the file is ready (often minutes, sometimes hours).
Go back to "Download your information", open the "Available downloads" (a.k.a. "Available copies") tab, tap "Download", and re-enter your Facebook password.
When the .zip finishes downloading, come back here and upload it. No need to unzip.
platforms.fileToUpload: One file named like facebook-<yourname>-<date>.zip (a few hundred MB up to a few GB). Inside: a your_facebook_activity/messages/ folder containing inbox/<person>/message_1.json files.
platforms.getItToPhone: The zip downloads straight to your phone (Downloads) — upload it from there, no transfer needed.
platforms.timeDelay: Usually minutes; can be up to 24–72 hours for large or old accounts.
caveats: The download option lives ONLY in the Facebook app now — the Messenger app no longer has it.
The Format selector defaults to HTML. Switch it to JSON every time; this is the #1 thing people miss.
The ready-to-download link expires after ~4 days — grab it promptly.
You'll re-enter your password before the download starts.
Huge accounts may be split into several zip parts — download and upload them all.

platforms.available: yes — facebook.com works the same way; also the ONLY place to export end-to-end encrypted chats
steps: STANDARD EXPORT (use this one): Go to facebook.com and log in.
Click your profile picture (top right), then "Settings & privacy", then "Settings".
In the left sidebar click "Accounts Center", then "Your information and permissions", then "Download your information".
Click "Download or transfer information", pick your account, and click "Next".
Choose "Some of your information", tick "Messages" only, click "Next".
Choose "Download to device". Set Date range = "All time", Format = "JSON" (default is HTML — switch it!), Media quality = "High", then click "Create files".
When notified it's ready, open the "Available downloads" tab, click "Download", re-enter your password, and save the .zip. Then move it to your phone (see below) and upload it here.

ENCRYPTED CHATS ONLY (do this ONLY if your conversation is end-to-end encrypted and didn't appear in the export above — desktop only): On a desktop browser go to facebook.com/messages (messenger.com was retired in April 2026 and now redirects here) and click your profile photo.
Click "Privacy & safety", then "End-to-end encrypted chats".
Click "Secure storage" (may be shown as "Message storage"). You must have set up a Secure Storage PIN / security method beforehand, or you'll be prompted to create one.
Click "Download secure storage data", then "Download file", and enter your Facebook password.
Save the resulting messages.zip, move it to your phone, and upload it here.
platforms.fileToUpload: Standard DYI export: facebook-<name>-<date>.zip (Messages, JSON). For E2EE chats only: a separate messages.zip from Secure storage.
platforms.getItToPhone: After it downloads to your computer: email the zip to yourself and open it on your phone, OR put it in Google Drive / iCloud Drive / Dropbox and download it in the phone's Files app, OR (Mac+iPhone) AirDrop it to your phone — then upload here from the phone.
platforms.timeDelay: Usually minutes; can be up to 24–72 hours for large accounts. (Secure-storage E2EE export is usually quick.)
caveats: The standard DYI export covers normal (non-encrypted) Messenger chats — that's what most people need.
End-to-end encrypted chats are NOT in the standard export. Their "Secure storage" export is desktop-only and produces a separate messages.zip.
Secure storage must be turned on (with a PIN/security method) before you can download from it.
messenger.com shut down in April 2026 — use facebook.com/messages on desktop for the Secure storage flow.
Marketplace chats, business chats and some group/community chats may be missing from the encrypted export (they aren't E2EE).
Whichever zip you get, upload it as-is — don't unzip it first.

generalCaveats: Do everything in the FACEBOOK app (or facebook.com), NOT the Messenger app — Messenger's own download/export option has been removed.
The Format selector DEFAULTS to HTML. You must switch it to JSON, otherwise the export won't import. This is the single most common mistake.
Choose "Some of your information" and tick only "Messages" — keeps the file small and the export fast.
Processing is usually a few minutes but can take up to 24–72 hours for large or very old accounts. You'll get a notification/email when ready.
The finished file appears under "Available downloads" (sometimes "Available copies") and the link expires after ~4 days — download it promptly.
You'll re-enter your Facebook password before the download starts.
Upload the .zip as-is — no need to unzip it.
Normal chats come out in the standard export; end-to-end encrypted chats need the separate, desktop-only "Secure storage" export at facebook.com/messages (messenger.com was retired April 2026).

troubleshooting.problem: I can't find a download/export option in the Messenger app.
troubleshooting.fix: It's not in Messenger anymore. Open the blue Facebook app instead: profile picture → Settings & privacy → Settings → Accounts Center → Your information and permissions → Download your information.

troubleshooting.problem: The export opened as web pages / it doesn't import.
troubleshooting.fix: You left the Format on HTML. Redo the export and change Format to JSON before tapping Create files. Echo needs the JSON version.

troubleshooting.problem: My export is huge or has been "creating" for hours / failed.
troubleshooting.fix: Make sure you picked "Some of your information" and ticked only "Messages". You can also narrow the Date range, or lower Media quality to Medium/Low. Large accounts can legitimately take up to 24–72 hours.

troubleshooting.problem: The download link doesn't work / says expired.
troubleshooting.fix: Download links expire after ~4 days. Go back to Download your information → Available downloads and create the export again, then download promptly. You'll re-enter your password.

troubleshooting.problem: A specific conversation is missing from the export.
troubleshooting.fix: That chat is probably end-to-end encrypted. Use the separate, desktop-only Secure storage export: on a desktop browser go to facebook.com/messages (messenger.com now redirects there) → profile photo → Privacy & safety → End-to-end encrypted chats → Secure storage → Download secure storage data. You must have a Secure Storage PIN set up first.

troubleshooting.problem: I exported on my computer and can't upload from there.
troubleshooting.fix: Echo is mobile-only. Get the zip onto your phone: email it to yourself, drop it in Google Drive / iCloud / Dropbox and open it in Files, or AirDrop it (Mac→iPhone). Then upload it here from your phone.

troubleshooting.problem: I got several zip files instead of one.
troubleshooting.fix: Very large accounts get split into multiple zip parts. Download all of them and upload them all — don't unzip them.

line.whatEchoNeeds: Upload the single .txt file that LINE's "Export chat history" produces on your phone (iPhone or Android) — it's already on your phone, so just pick it from Files/Downloads when Echo asks. The one thing that matters: before you export, scroll the conversation all the way up to the oldest message (LINE only saves what has been loaded), and do NOT use the LINE desktop app's "Save chat" — that file is a different format Echo can't read.

platforms.available: yes — fully on-device, no computer needed
steps: Open the LINE app and tap the chat (1-to-1 or group) you want to turn into a persona.

Load the FULL history first: scroll all the way up to the very first/oldest message. LINE only exports messages that have been loaded on screen, so anything you don't scroll to is missing. For long chats keep scrolling until no older messages appear.
Tap the menu icon at the top-right of the chat — three stacked horizontal lines (☰).
Tap "Settings" (the gear). In some versions this opens the chat's own settings screen directly.

Scroll down and tap "Export chat history". (LINE has no with-media / without-media choice — the file is always text only; stickers, photos and attachments are never included, which is exactly what Echo wants.)
The iOS Share sheet opens. Easiest: tap "Save to Files", pick a spot you'll remember (e.g. "On My iPhone" or iCloud Drive → Downloads), then "Save". Or tap "Mail" and email it to yourself.
Open Echo, choose LINE, tap upload/attach, and pick that .txt from the Files app.
Done — the export is instant and never leaves your phone until you upload it.
platforms.fileToUpload: A single plain-text file named like [LINE] Chat with <name>.txt (TAB-separated, UTF-8). No zip, no media — text only.
platforms.getItToPhone: Not needed — the export happens on the phone. Save it to Files/Downloads and upload from there.
caveats: LINE only saves messages currently loaded — skip the scroll-to-top step and older history is silently left out.
The .txt has no photos, voice notes, stickers or files — normal and fine for Echo.
This text export cannot be re-imported back into LINE later; it's an archive copy.
For a very large chat the Share sheet may take a few seconds to produce the file — wait for it.
Use the ☰ icon at the very top-right of the open chat, not LINE's home screen.

platforms.available: yes — fully on-device, no computer needed
steps: Open the LINE app and tap the chat (1-to-1 or group) you want to turn into a persona.

Load the FULL history first: scroll all the way up to the oldest message. LINE only exports what has been loaded on screen, so un-scrolled history won't be saved. Keep going until nothing older loads.
Tap the menu icon at the top-right of the chat — three stacked horizontal lines (☰).
Tap "Settings". If you don't see the export option directly, tap "Other settings" first — on some Android builds "Export chat history" lives one level deeper.
Tap "Export chat history". (LINE has no with/without-media option — the file is always text only.)
The Android share/save sheet opens. Tap "Save to Drive" / "Files" / "Save" to store it on the phone (e.g. Downloads), or share it to Gmail to yourself.
If you saved to Downloads, the file is now in your phone's storage under Downloads.
Open Echo, choose LINE, tap upload/attach, and pick that .txt (look in Downloads or wherever you saved it).
Done — the export is instant and stays on your phone until you upload it.
platforms.fileToUpload: A single plain-text file named like [LINE] Chat with <name>.txt (TAB-separated, UTF-8). No zip, no media — text only.
platforms.getItToPhone: Not needed — the export happens on the phone. Save it to Downloads/Drive and upload from there.
caveats: Only messages loaded in the chat are exported — skipping scroll-to-top silently drops older history.
No media in the file (photos, stickers, voice, attachments) — normal and fine for Echo.
The .txt cannot be restored back into LINE; it's an archive copy.
Wording varies by Android version/region: Settings → Export chat history, or Settings → Other settings → Export chat history.
Use the ☰ menu inside the open chat, not the gear on LINE's home/settings tab.

platforms.available: no — do NOT use for Echo. The LINE desktop app's "Save chat" produces a different (space-separated) .txt that Echo cannot parse
steps: Skip this method. Echo needs the phone export, not the desktop one.
On Windows/Mac, the LINE app's menu (3-dots, top-right of the chat) offers "Save chat", but that file uses spaces between fields, not tabs, so Echo can't read it.
Instead, open the LINE app on your iPhone or Android, open the same chat, and follow the phone steps.
The phone export saves straight to your phone — no AirDrop, email or Drive transfer needed.
platforms.fileToUpload: None — skip desktop. Export from your phone instead (the phone export is TAB-separated; desktop "Save chat" is space-separated and incompatible).
platforms.getItToPhone: Don't export on the computer. The phone export is already on your phone, so there's nothing to transfer. If your history is only on desktop, open the same chat in the LINE phone app and export there.
caveats: Desktop "Save chat" = wrong format for Echo (space-delimited). Phone "Export chat history" = correct format (TAB-delimited).
If a chat exists on both desktop and phone, always export from the phone for Echo.

generalCaveats: LINE export is 100% on-device on both iPhone and Android — you never need a computer, and the file never leaves your phone until you upload it.
Scroll to the very top of the chat BEFORE exporting. LINE only saves messages it has loaded on screen; un-scrolled older history is silently left out — the #1 cause of an incomplete persona.
The export is text only. Photos, voice messages, stickers and files are never in the .txt — expected and exactly what Echo uses.
LINE has NO with-media / without-media choice (that's WhatsApp). Don't go looking for it — just tap "Export chat history".
Don't use the LINE desktop app's "Save chat" — it makes a space-separated file Echo can't read. The phone's "Export chat history" makes the correct TAB-separated file.
The file is a plain .txt (not a .zip) and is UTF-8 encoded, so accented characters, Japanese, Thai, etc. come through correctly.
This text export can't be restored back into LINE later — it's an archive copy, safe to share with Echo.

troubleshooting.problem: My persona is missing old messages / only the last few weeks came through.
troubleshooting.fix: LINE only exports what was loaded on screen. Re-open the chat, scroll all the way up to the very first message (until nothing older appears), THEN tap menu (☰) → Settings → Export chat history again, and upload the new file.

troubleshooting.problem: I can't find "Export chat history" in the menu.
troubleshooting.fix: Open the menu from INSIDE the chat (the ☰ icon at the top-right of the conversation, not LINE's home screen). On Android, tap "Settings" then look for "Other settings" — the export option can be one level deeper. Update LINE if the menu looks different.

troubleshooting.problem: Echo says the file format is wrong / can't read my LINE file.
troubleshooting.fix: You probably exported from the LINE desktop app ("Save chat"), which is space-separated and unsupported. Re-export from the LINE app on your phone using "Export chat history" — that produces the TAB-separated .txt Echo needs.

troubleshooting.problem: There are no photos or stickers in the export.
troubleshooting.fix: Normal — LINE never includes media in the text export. Echo only needs the text, so the file is complete as-is.

troubleshooting.problem: Characters look like garbled symbols (mojibake) when I peek at the file.
troubleshooting.fix: The file is UTF-8. Some basic text viewers guess the wrong encoding — that's just the preview, not the file. Upload it as-is; Echo reads UTF-8 correctly. Avoid re-saving it in another encoding before uploading.

troubleshooting.problem: The Share sheet hangs or no file appears after I tap Export.
troubleshooting.fix: Very large chats take a few seconds to build the file — wait for the Share/Save sheet to finish. If it stalls, close and re-open the chat, confirm you have free storage, and try again.

vk.whatEchoNeeds: Upload the whole VK data archive .zip (the official one from "Запросить архив"); leave it zipped — Echo reads the messages/ HTML files inside it. The one thing that matters: make sure "Сообщения" / Messages is included (tick it on the computer/full-site version for the smallest, fastest export).

platforms.available: yes — RECOMMENDED. The computer (full vk.com site) is the only place you can reliably tick "Messages" specifically so the export stays small and fast
steps: Open vk.com in a web browser on your computer and log in.

Go straight to the data export page: type vk.com/data_protection?section=rules in the address bar (or: profile picture top-right → "Настройки" / Settings → "Безопасность и конфиденциальность" / "Приватность" → scroll to the "Управление данными" / Data Management block).
Scroll to the bottom and click "Запросить архив" / "Запросить архив данных" (Request Archive).

On the selection screen you'll see categories (Профиль / Profile, Платежи / Payments, Реклама / Ads, Прочее / Other). Turn OFF everything you don't need, and make sure "Сообщения" (Messages) — under the "Профиль" / Profile group — is turned ON (checkbox checked).

IMPORTANT: leave "комментарии" / comments and other heavy items unchecked — the page shows an estimated build time, and adding comments can push it from a few hours to a couple of days. Messages-only is the fastest.
Click the request/confirm button and confirm with the one-time code VK sends (SMS or in-app). The download link is tied to your profile and can't be opened from another account.
Wait for VK to build the archive. Messages-only is often ready in about 2–3 hours, but VK quotes up to a few days for large accounts — you don't need to keep the page open.

When ready you'll get a bell-icon notification and a private message from "Администрация ВКонтакте". Open it and click "Скачать архив" / "Скачать файл". A .zip downloads. The link is valid ~7 days, so don't wait too long.
Move the .zip to your phone (see "Get it to phone"), then upload it to Echo.
platforms.fileToUpload: The VK data archive .zip (e.g. archive.zip). Inside it is HTML: a top-level index.html / index-messages.html and a messages/ folder with one subfolder per dialog containing messages*.html files (Cyrillic windows-1251 encoding). Upload the whole .zip to Echo as-is — do not unzip it.
platforms.getItToPhone: After the .zip lands on your computer: (1) iPhone — AirDrop the .zip from your Mac/PC to your iPhone, then in Echo's upload picker choose "Files" / Browse and pick it. (2) Any phone — email the .zip to yourself and open the attachment, or upload it to Google Drive / iCloud Drive / Dropbox and download it into your phone's Files app. Keep it zipped — Echo wants the original .zip, not the extracted folder.
platforms.timeDelay: Usually ~2–3 hours for a Messages-only archive on a normal account; VK can take up to a few days for very large accounts or if you included comments/other heavy data.
caveats: This is the only path where you can reliably pick "Сообщения" / Messages on its own — do it here, not in the phone app.
Don't open the VK download link inside the VK app — it won't download. Open it in a real browser (Chrome, Safari, Edge).

Menu wording varies by VK version: the block may read "Безопасность и конфиденциальность", "Приватность", or "Управление данными" — the button always says "Запросить архив" / "Запросить архив данных".
The archive is a snapshot at the moment it's built — chats you deleted earlier won't be in it (VK doesn't keep deleted messages).

platforms.available: partial — works from a phone browser at m.vk.com / vk.com, but the data-selection screen is often only offered on the full desktop site; on the phone you may be unable to pick Messages-only
steps: On your phone, open a browser (Safari or Chrome) and go to m.vk.com or vk.com — do NOT use the VK app for this part.
Log in to your VK account.
Go to the data export page directly: vk.com/data_protection?section=rules (or menu → "Настройки" / Settings → "Приватность" / Privacy → "Управление данными" / Data Management).
Scroll to the bottom and tap "Запросить архив" / "Запросить архив данных" (Request Archive).

If you're shown the data-selection screen, turn off everything except "Сообщения" / Messages (under "Профиль" / Profile). If the mobile page builds the whole archive without letting you choose, that's fine too — Messages are still included; the file is just bigger. For a small file, use a computer instead.
Confirm with the one-time code VK sends (SMS or notification).
Wait for the ready notification (bell icon + a message from "Администрация ВКонтакте"). When it arrives, open it IN THE BROWSER (not the VK app) and tap "Скачать архив" / "Скачать файл".
The .zip saves to your phone's Downloads / Files. In Echo, tap upload, choose "Files" / Browse, and select that .zip.
platforms.fileToUpload: The same VK data archive .zip (HTML inside, messages/ folder, windows-1251). Upload the whole .zip — don't unzip it.
platforms.getItToPhone: Not needed if you did it in the phone browser — the .zip is already in Downloads/Files. Just upload it from there.
platforms.timeDelay: Same as desktop — typically a few hours for Messages-only, up to a few days for large accounts.
caveats: Use the mobile BROWSER, not the VK app — the app can't open the secure download link, so the file won't save.

On the phone the selection screen often doesn't appear and VK builds the whole archive at once (no way to pick just Messages). It still contains your messages; the .zip is just larger. For a small file, do it on a computer.
If the .zip won't open on the phone, you don't need to open it — just hand the .zip straight to Echo. Echo reads inside it for you.

generalCaveats: VK only has an official "request my data" export — there's no instant in-chat export, so you request the archive and wait for VK to build it (often a few hours, sometimes days).
The export is the OFFICIAL VK data archive, not a third-party tool. Get it from Settings → Data Management → "Запросить архив" (or vk.com/data_protection?section=rules).
Inside the .zip, messages are HTML files in windows-1251 (Cyrillic) encoding inside a messages/ folder — that's normal and Echo handles it. Just upload the .zip whole.
The archive is a point-in-time snapshot: messages you deleted before requesting it are gone and won't appear.
The download link is single-use, tied to your account, valid ~7 days, and must be opened in a real web browser — never inside the VK app.
Picking just "Сообщения" / Messages (best done on the full desktop site) keeps the file small and the build fast; including comments or everything can stretch it to a day or more.
Privacy note: a full archive can contain payments, ads and profile data too — if you only want Echo to see your chats, export Messages only.

troubleshooting.problem: I can't find "Запросить архив" in Settings.
troubleshooting.fix: Go directly to vk.com/data_protection?section=rules and scroll to the very bottom — the button is at the end of the page. Surrounding wording may say "Управление данными", "Безопасность и конфиденциальность" or "Приватность" depending on your VK version.

troubleshooting.problem: The download link doesn't do anything / the file won't save on my phone.
troubleshooting.fix: You're probably opening it inside the VK app. Open the VK message in Safari/Chrome instead — the secure download only works in a real browser.

troubleshooting.problem: On my phone I couldn't choose just Messages.
troubleshooting.fix: The mobile page often builds the whole archive with no selection. That's OK — your messages are still inside; the file's just bigger. For a small Messages-only file, request it from a computer at vk.com.

troubleshooting.problem: The archive is taking forever (a day or more).
troubleshooting.fix: You likely included comments or all data. Wait it out, or request again with ONLY "Сообщения" / Messages ticked — that build is usually a few hours.

troubleshooting.problem: The link expired before I downloaded it.
troubleshooting.fix: The link is valid ~7 days and is single-use. Go back and click "Запросить архив" again to generate a fresh archive and link.

troubleshooting.problem: Text in the messages looks like garbled symbols when I peek inside the zip.
troubleshooting.fix: That's the windows-1251 encoding showing wrong in a preview — expected. Don't try to fix it; just upload the original .zip to Echo, which decodes it correctly.
